summary: Recording quality not specified, but the show shines with a smoking Samson, Terrapin Station and an extended Space jam.
review: All three reviewers praise this late‑1984 performance as one of their favorites, noting a well‑played set that includes a scorching "Samson & Delilah," a soaring "Terrapin Station" and a long, atmospheric Space. The concert is described as emotionally resonant—particularly the tear‑jerking "Brokedown Palace" after news of Steve Parish’s family tragedy—and features standout drumming throughout.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong, consistent praise for the musical performance (70% weight) combined with no specific recording comments leads to an overall high rating despite uncertain technical quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers do not mention sound clarity, mix balance, or source type, so recording quality cannot be assessed from the comments.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers describe the entire concert as well‑played with strong pacing, noting a somber yet powerful second set and extended improvisational sections.

band_member_comments.Drums: Long drum jams highlighted; reviewers note amazing playing and extended drums during Space.
